stories.headline: What does Trump shooting at US dinner mean for World Cup security?
stories.summary: Football fans criticise security arrangements in World Cup cohost nation after assassination attempt on US president.

stories.summary: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

stories.headline: Analysis: Bennett-Lapid alliance attempt to unseat Israel’s Netanyahu
stories.summary: Bennett and Lapid join forces again in a renewed bid for power that will, analysts warn, mean little for Palestinians.

stories.headline: Germany’s Merz says US has no strategy in Iran war
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US has no clear exit strategy with their war on Iran.

stories.headline: NPT summit: Can nuclear pact survive US-Israel war on Iran?
stories.summary: Analysts say a review conference is unlikely to halt nuclear powers from continuing to flout the treaty's rules.

stories.headline: Trump to hold talks on Iran with senior security officials, US media say
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Tehran has blamed the US for the failure of talks after Trump decided against sending a negotiating team to Pakistan at the weekend.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack

stories.headline: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war

stories.summary: Regulators had reviewed whether deal violated Beijing’s investment rules

stories.headline: Security at Correspondents’ Dinner Worked as Intended, Experts Say
stories.summary: Officials noted that the suspect never made it into the hotel ballroom, where President Trump and hundreds of journalists were gathered for the White House correspondents’ dinner.

stories.headline: Merz suggests Ukraine’s EU membership may depend on territorial concessions
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Monday that Ukraine’s proposed timeline for EU membership is unrealistic, as debate over enlargement continues to intensify. “Zelenskyy had the idea of joining the EU on January 1, 2027. That will not work. Even January 1, 2028, is not realistic."

stories.headline: King Charles’s rare state visit offers U.K. a chance to mend ties with Trump
stories.summary: The pageantry begins today amid heightened security concerns and a growing rift over the Iran war. The U.K. hopes the president’s love of pomp and the king’s “poker face” can help heal their alliance.

stories.headline: Lebanon Latest: Israel’s deadly attacks amid ceasefire breaches
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes hit southern Lebanon as ceasefire falters, killing at least 14 and forcing new displacement.

stories.summary: Thousands of seafarers are stranded as the Iran war shuts down much of the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global oil and gas trade and raising safety fears.

stories.headline: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit
stories.summary: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church of England, had a private discussion with Pope Leo during the trip.

stories.headline: Firms Swarm US Market With New Debt, Seizing Issuance Window
stories.summary: Firms swarmed the US primary markets with new debt on Monday, seizing a window to lock in borrowing costs ahead of a raft of earnings and central bank decisions, even as uncertainty in the Middle East lingers.

stories.headline: RBC Hires Graham, Boccio as Heads of CDS and Electronic Trading
stories.summary: Royal Bank of Canada is bolstering its credit trading business with senior hires, including new roles focused on credit-default swaps and electronic trading.

stories.headline: Correspondents’ dinner lacked highest security level despite presence of top officials
stories.summary: The White House correspondents’ dinner, which was attended by the president and many Cabinet members, was not given top security status that would have unlocked the full weight of federal resources.

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed amid flurry of insurgent attacks
stories.summary: Car bomb kills Sadio Camara at home during coordinated assaults by rebel groups including West African al-Qaida affiliateMali’s defence minister was killed in an attack on his residence, the government said on Sunday, a high-profile fatality during coordinated assaults staged the previous day by insurgents including the West African affiliate of al-Qaida.A car laden with explosives driven by a suicide attacker drove into Sadio Camara’s residence

stories.headline: Italy’s Angelini Said to Explore Catalyst Pharma Acquisition

stories.headline: Stonepeak and Bernhard to Buy Cleco From Macquarie-Led Group
stories.summary: Stonepeak and Bernhard Capital Partners agreed to buy Cleco Group LLC from a consortium comprised of Macquarie Asset Management, British Columbia Investment Management Corporation and Manulife Investment Management.

stories.headline: Haas Boss on F1 US Boom
stories.summary: Haas F1 Team Principal Ayao Komatsu joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down Formula 1’s explosive US growth, the controversial new regulations, and how team culture is driving their success this season. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Vitol US Gasoline Traders Greer, Reiser Depart From Energy Giant
stories.summary: Two US gasoline traders have departed from Vitol Group, the world’s largest independent commodity trading house.

stories.headline: Banks Launch $2 Billion BASF Coatings Loan Sale for Buyout
stories.summary: Banks have launched a debt sale worth over $2 billion for the auto paint and finishing division of BASF SE, amid a difficult market backdrop for the chemicals industry.

stories.headline: India Changes Bank Loan-Loss Rules to Align With Global Norms
stories.summary: India’s central bank has introduced tighter rules requiring banks to set aside more funds for future losses on credit and loan portfolios, in a move to align the sector’s norms with global standards.

stories.headline: Hongqiao Said to Price $1.5 Billion Convertibles Near Low End
stories.summary: China Hongqiao Group Ltd. priced its 10.2 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) sale of bonds that can be converted into stock near the low end of the marketed range.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Pressures Democrats to Reject Super PAC Support in Primaries

stories.headline: EU faces ‘China shock’ as EV imports drive Beijing’s record surplus with bloc
stories.summary: China sold goods worth about $148bn to EU in first quarter of year, but imported just $65bnThe EU is experiencing a prolonged “China shock” as a flood of Chinese EVs into Europe helped push Beijing to a record surplus with the bloc.New data showed China’s trade surplus – where its exports to the EU exceeded imports from the bloc – was $83bn (£61bn) in the first three months of 2026. stories.headline: Fighting over access to water in Chad kills at least 42, army intervenes
stories.summary: Dispute over water spirals into reprisal attacks, government says situation is 'under control'.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat

stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested

stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?

stories.headline: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
